summary: clier dev-01e85e9 holds together end-to-end: the help surface, every team CRUD step (create/get/update/star/unstar/reset-protocol/delete with subteam cascade-protection), the full run lifecycle (start/list/view/tell/capture/stop), and the canonical tutorial walkthrough all behave as documented. Errors print on stderr in the documented `error: <status> <title>: <detail>` shape (404/403/409/422 surface their detail), client-side rejections share the same single-line stderr shape, multi-field validation aggregates every offender with `; ` separators, idempotent operations (star/unstar) stay idempotent, JSON output uses `[]` (never null) for empty collections, RFC3339 timestamps carry their offset, and the run dir under `~/.clier/runs/<id>/` is cleanly removed by `run stop`. The two stray-positional defects from the prior run (`clier version foo`, `clier team list jakeraft`) are fixed in this build — both now reject with `error: unknown command "…" for "clier …"` and exit 1. One new fail surfaced: `clier run stop --help` advertises the command as idempotent ("running it on an already-stopped run just clears any leftover dir"), but a second `run stop` on the same id returns `error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs` with exit 1 — documented behaviour and actual behaviour disagree. Tutorial step 5 (`run attach`) was skipped because it requires an interactive TTY. Cross-coverage between the free-form pass and the tutorial walkthrough is otherwise clean except that team unstar / team reset-protocol / team delete / error-path matrix are exercised by free-form but never demonstrated in the tutorial — captured as a single coverage finding.

findings.title: Top-level --help recurses cleanly into every subcommand
findings.rationale: Starting from `clier --help` a new agent sees the Available Commands list (auth, open, run, team, tutorial, version) plus the canonical entry points 'First run: clier tutorial' and 'Per command: clier <command> --help'. Recursing into each subcommand's help — including team's eight leaves and run's seven — every command is reachable, every flag is documented, and required-flag annotations on `team create` declare exactly what the wire layer needs.
findings.stdout_excerpt:
First run:  clier tutorial
Per command: clier <command> --help
...
Available Commands:
  auth, open, run, team, tutorial, version

findings.title: Unknown commands and flags reject with single-line stderr and exit 1
findings.rationale: `clier nonsense`, `clier team nonsense`, and `clier team list --bogus` all print exactly one line to stderr starting with 'error: ', leave stdout empty, and exit non-zero — the same single-line shape promised by the top-level help text 'Errors print one line on stderr starting with "error: " and exit non-zero — the same single-line shape across server validation, client-side validation, and unknown commands.'

steps.label: unknown top-level command
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "nonsense" for "clier"

steps.label: unknown subcommand
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "nonsense" for "clier team"

steps.label: unknown flag
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown flag: --bogus

findings.title: Stray positional args on `clier version` and `clier team list` are now rejected
findings.rationale: Both fails recorded in the prior run (qa-20260507-064322-b662abb3) are fixed in dev-01e85e9: `clier version foo` and `clier team list jakeraft` now print `error: unknown command "…" for "clier …"` to stderr and exit 1. Strictness is consistent with `clier team get jakeraft/hello-clier extra` which still returns `error: expected exactly one <namespace/name>, got 2`.

steps.label: clier version foo
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "foo" for "clier version"

steps.label: clier team list jakeraft
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: unknown command "jakeraft" for "clier team list"

steps.label: clier team get extra arg still rejected
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: expected exactly one <namespace/name>, got 2

findings.title: Team create / get / update / patch-json / reset-protocol / delete round-trip
findings.rationale: Created `jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude` with required flags only, read it back via get (server-default protocol injected), updated description with `--description`, replaced protocol with `--patch-json '{"protocol":"…"}'`, restored default with `team reset-protocol`, deleted, and re-read returns 404 — every mutation is queryable through the read-side surface and every reversal lands back on the prior shape. `updated_at` advances with each mutation, `created_at` stays pinned.

steps.label: create
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude --agent-type claude --command 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' --git-repo-url https://github.com/jakeraft/hello-clier --description 'QA disposable team'

steps.label: update --description
steps.command: clier team update jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude --description 'QA disposable team — updated'

steps.label: update --patch-json protocol
steps.command:
clier team update jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude --patch-json '{"protocol":"# Custom protocol

You are **{{self}}**.
"}'

steps.label: reset-protocol restores server default
steps.stdout_excerpt: protocol back to server default '# Team Protocol\n\nYou are **{{self}}** in team **{{root_team}}**...'

steps.label: get after delete returns 404
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude not found

findings.title: team star / unstar are idempotent and caller-aware
findings.rationale: Calling `team star` twice in a row on `jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude` returns the same `{"starred":true}` response both times — the second call is a no-op, no '409 already starred'. Same for `team unstar`. After star+star the read-side `star.count` reads 1 (not 2), confirming the second call did not double-count. After unstar+unstar count reads 0.

findings.title: team delete refuses to drop a team referenced as a subteam
findings.rationale: Created child `jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude-child`, attached it via `team update --subteam`, attempted to delete the child while still referenced — server returned `error: 409 Failed precondition: team is referenced as a subteam by another team` (exit 1). Cleared the parent's subteams with `--subteam ''`, retried `team delete` — succeeded. Wire-level invariant from `team delete --help` ('referencing team must drop the link first') is enforced.

steps.label: delete while referenced is rejected
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Failed precondition: team is referenced as a subteam by another team

findings.title: Server-side 422 / 403 / 404 / 409 errors render in `error: <status> <title>: <detail>` shape
findings.rationale: Across team create / update / get / delete / star / reset-protocol the four documented server status families surface with one stderr line each — the wire shape promised by `clier --help` ('Errors print one line on stderr starting with "error: " and exit non-zero — the same single-line shape across server validation, client-side validation, and unknown commands.'). 422 carries the field-level detail, 403 names the namespace, 404 names the missing key, 409 explains the precondition.

steps.label: 422 bad agent_type
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/error-probe-2 --agent-type bogus --command x --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"

steps.label: 422 non-https git URL
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/error-probe-3 --agent-type claude --command x --git-repo-url git@github.com:x/y.git
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: git_repo_url: must be a GitHub HTTPS URL (https://github.com/owner/repo)

steps.label: 403 foreign namespace on create
steps.command: clier team create someone-else/x --agent-type claude --command x --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 403 Permission denied: caller "jakeraft" does not own namespace "someone-else"

steps.label: 404 team not found on get
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 404 Resource not found: team jakeraft/nonexistent-zzz not found

steps.label: 409 duplicate create
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude --agent-type claude --command x --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 409 Resource already exists: team jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude already exists

steps.label: 422 immutable agent_type via patch-json
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: is not patchable (immutable or unknown)

steps.label: 422 protocol null rejected
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: protocol: must not be null (use "" for empty value, or :reset-protocol for default protocol)

findings.title: Client-side validation errors share the same single-line stderr shape
findings.rationale: Missing flags, invalid namespace/name strings, empty arg lists all reject locally before any wire call — the message starts with `error: `, lands on stderr only, and exits 1. The shape mirrors server-side validation: agent code does not need a different parser for client vs server failures.

steps.label: missing required flags
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "agent-type", "command", "git-repo-url" not set

steps.label: invalid team ID (no slash)
steps.command: clier team create no-slash --agent-type claude --command x --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: invalid team ID "no-slash" (expected namespace/name)

steps.label: empty name half
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/ --agent-type claude --command x --git-repo-url https://github.com/x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: invalid team ID "jakeraft/" (expected namespace/name)

steps.label: team get without arg
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: <namespace/name> is required

steps.label: team update with no fields
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: no fields to update — pass at least one --description / --command / --git-repo-url / --git-subpath / --subteam / --patch-json

findings.title: Multi-field 422 violations surface every offender in one line
findings.rationale: Trust 3 says 'Multi-field violations surface every offender in one response — no short-circuit on the first one.' Verified on both create and list: passing two or three bad inputs yields a single 422 line with all offenders joined by `; `, no early exit on the first one. An agent does not need to retry to discover the second or third issue.

steps.label: two bad fields on create
steps.command: clier team create jakeraft/multi-violator --agent-type bogus --command x --git-repo-url ftp://x/y
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"; git_repo_url: must be a GitHub HTTPS URL (https://github.com/owner/repo)

steps.label: three bad params on list
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: sort: must be one of "stars_desc", "stars_asc", "updated_desc", "updated_asc"; agent_type: must be one of "claude", "codex"; page_size: must not exceed 100

findings.title: team list bounds (page_size 1..100, q ≤200, opaque cursor) all enforce
findings.rationale: Each documented bound on `team list` rejects with a 422 line naming the offending field. `--page-size 0` and `--page-size 200` both reject (lower-bound message says 'must be between 1 and 100', upper-bound says 'must not exceed 100' — slightly different wording but each accurate); `--q` over 200 chars rejects; opaque `--page-token garbage` rejects.

steps.label: page-size lower bound
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must be between 1 and 100

steps.label: page-size upper bound
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_size: must not exceed 100

steps.label: q length cap
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: q: exceeds 200 character cap

steps.label: bad cursor
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: 422 Invalid argument: page_token: invalid cursor

findings.title: team list returns `[]` (not null) on empty data and RFC3339 timestamps
findings.rationale: `clier team list --namespace zzz-nonexistent` returns `{"data": [], "meta": {"has_next": false, "next_cursor": ""}}` — empty array as `[]` not null, empty string for next_cursor not null. Populated `team get` records carry `created_at`/`updated_at` as RFC3339 with offset (e.g. `2026-05-07T16:41:52.74812+09:00`), and field names (`namespace`, `name`, `agent_type`, `subteams`, `star.starred`, `star.count`) stayed stable across every read in this run.

findings.title: run start / list / view / capture / tell / stop round-trip
findings.rationale: Started a fresh run on `jakeraft/hello-clier`, captured run_id, listed (newest first, two agents recorded with the QA-lead self-run), viewed (full plan + protocols + args + empty messages), captured both panes (RFC3339 captured_at + content), sent three tells (positional / stdin pipe / --from peer), re-read run.json — all three messages persisted with timestamps, `from: null` for the no-`--from` calls and `from: "jakeraft.hello-codex"` for the `--from` call. Stop returned `{"stopped":true}`, the dir under `~/.clier/runs/20260507-074351-bb3e694e/` was removed, run list dropped that id, run view returns 404.

steps.label: run start
steps.stdout_excerpt: run_id=20260507-074351-bb3e694e session_name=clier-… agents=[hello-clier window 0, hello-codex window 1]

steps.label: run list shows new run + qa-lead self
run capture both panes
steps.stdout_excerpt: data=[{agent_id=jakeraft.hello-clier, captured_at=2026-05-07T16:44:03.193001+09:00, content=Claude Code v2.1.132 …}, {agent_id=jakeraft.hello-codex, captured_at=…, content=OpenAI Codex (v0.128.0) …}]

steps.label: run tell positional
steps.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-074351-bb3e694e --to jakeraft.hello-clier 'hello from QA — ignore this and exit'

steps.label: run tell stdin pipe (multi-line)
steps.command: echo -e 'line1\nline2\nignore please' | clier run tell --run 20260507-074351-bb3e694e --to jakeraft.hello-clier

steps.label: run tell --from peer
steps.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-074351-bb3e694e --to jakeraft.hello-clier --from jakeraft.hello-codex 'from peer'

steps.label: run view records all three messages
run dir removed
steps.stderr_excerpt: ls: /Users/jake_kakao/.clier/runs/20260507-074351-bb3e694e/: No such file or directory

steps.label: run view after stop = 404
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

findings.title: `run stop` is not idempotent — re-stopping returns error 1, contradicting `run stop --help`
findings.rationale: `clier run stop --help` claims 'Stop is final and idempotent: running it on an already-stopped run just clears any leftover dir.' But after a successful stop on `20260507-074447-d7e20e61` (which removed the run dir), a second `clier run stop 20260507-074447-d7e20e61` returns `error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs` and exits 1 — not a no-op. An automation that retries stop after a transient failure (or that issues stop defensively at the end of a script) will see a spurious failure even though the desired state has already been reached.
findings.fail_note: Either make `run stop` truly idempotent — exit 0 with `{"stopped":true,"already_stopped":true}` (or equivalent) when the dir is already gone — or amend the help text to drop the idempotency claim and say the second call returns 404. Prefer the first; the docstring promise is the better contract.

references.label: clier run stop --help
references.excerpt: Stop is final and idempotent: running it on an already-stopped run just clears any leftover dir.

findings.title: Local-state run commands all reject unknown ids with the same single-line stderr
findings.rationale: `run view` / `run capture` / `run tell` / `run attach` / `run stop` against a fabricated `fake-run-id-zzz` each emit `error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs` on stderr and exit 1. Per-command client-side missing-args checks (`<run-id> is required`, `required flag(s) "run", "to" not set`) follow the same shape. `run capture --agent jakeraft.nonexistent-agent` and `run tell --to jakeraft.nonexistent-agent` against a real run report `error: agent not found in run: <id>` (exit 1).

steps.label: run view unknown
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

steps.label: run tell missing flags
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: required flag(s) "run", "to" not set

steps.label: run capture missing arg
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: <run-id> is required

steps.label: run attach unknown id
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: run not found in ~/.clier/runs

steps.label: run tell unknown agent on live run
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: agent not found in run: jakeraft.nonexistent-agent

steps.label: run capture --agent unknown
steps.stderr_excerpt: error: agent not found in run: jakeraft.nonexistent-agent

findings.title: run stop removes the entire ~/.clier/runs/<id>/ directory — Trust 6 holds
findings.rationale: Before stop: `~/.clier/runs/20260507-074351-bb3e694e/` contains `jakeraft.hello-clier/`, `jakeraft.hello-codex/`, `protocols/`, and `run.json` (2785 bytes). After `clier run stop 20260507-074351-bb3e694e`: the dir is gone — `ls` reports 'No such file or directory'. No orphaned clones, no stale run.json. `clier run list` excludes the id. ADR-0004 §4.6 contract is upheld.
findings.stderr_excerpt: ls: /Users/jake_kakao/.clier/runs/20260507-074351-bb3e694e/: No such file or directory

findings.title: team delete removes the server-side row — disposable prefix cleanly removed
findings.rationale: After `team delete jakeraft/qa-20260507074030-claude` the namespace listing (`team list --namespace jakeraft`) contains only the operator's pre-existing rows: `hello-clier`, `clier-qa-lead`, `hello-codex`. No `qa-20260507074030-claude*` orphans. A subsequent `team get` of either deleted id returns 404 — Trust 6 holds.
findings.stdout_excerpt: data=[hello-clier, clier-qa-lead, hello-codex] — no qa-20260507074030-claude rows

findings.title: open dashboard returns the configured URL as JSON
findings.rationale: `clier version` advertises `dashboard_url=http://localhost:5173`; `clier open dashboard` returns `{"opened":"http://localhost:5173"}` (exit 0). Round-trip with `clier version` is consistent. The `--help` documents `CLIER_DASHBOARD_URL` for per-process override and the per-process call honours the active env var.

findings.title: Tutorial steps 1–4 + 6 reproduce as documented; step 5 is interactive
findings.rationale: Walked the canonical tutorial verbatim. Step 1 `team list` returns the catalog (jakeraft/hello-clier first by stars_desc). Step 2 `run start jakeraft/hello-clier` mints a fresh run and prints run_id `20260507-074447-d7e20e61`. Step 3 the heredoc form `clier run tell --run <id> --to jakeraft.hello-clier <<'EOF' …` accepts multi-line stdin and persists the message. Step 4 `run capture` returns both pane buffers as JSON without attaching. Step 6 `run stop` tears the run down and clears `~/.clier/runs/<id>/`. Step 5 (`run attach`) is a TTY-only interactive flow — recorded as skip per the skill instructions.

steps.label: Step 3: run tell heredoc multi-line
steps.command: clier run tell --run 20260507-074447-d7e20e61 --to jakeraft.hello-clier <<'EOF'\nGreet your peer and tell me what you learned.\nEOF

steps.label: Step 4: run capture
steps.stdout_excerpt: data=[{agent_id:jakeraft.hello-clier,content:'…❯ Greet your peer and tell me what you learned. …'},{agent_id:jakeraft.hello-codex,content:'…OpenAI Codex (v0.128.0)…'}]

steps.label: Step 5: run attach
steps.skip_reason: Requires an interactive TTY; the skill explicitly instructs to record skip and continue. The capture step in §4 covers the read-side need without a TTY.

findings.title: Tutorial does not exercise team unstar / reset-protocol / delete or any failure path
findings.rationale: Cross-coverage check: the §2 free-form pass covered team unstar, team reset-protocol, team delete (with subteam-cascade-protection), and the full failure-path matrix (422 / 403 / 404 / 409, multi-field aggregation, missing required flags, immutable agent_type, protocol null, page-size bounds, q-cap, opaque cursor) — none of which appear in `clier tutorial`. The tutorial deliberately constrains itself to the shortest-path-to-a-running-run. This is a coverage observation, not a defect: the tutorial promises a five-minute happy path and delivers one. Recorded for transparency rather than as a bug. Inverse direction (tutorial steps not exercised by free-form) is empty.

references.label: clier tutorial — full body
references.excerpt: 1. clier team list … 2. clier run start jakeraft/hello-clier … 3. clier run tell … 4. clier run capture … 5. clier run attach … 6. clier run stop … Going further: Author your own team: clier team create --help. (No mention of unstar/reset-protocol/delete or any failure path.)
